howItWorks.intro: This anchor rode calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Rode Length = (Water Depth + Bow Height) × Scope Ratio
Chain = at least 1 boat length or 15% of rode
Swing Radius = Rode Length + Boat Length
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is anchor scope?
faq.answer: Scope is the ratio of anchor rode length to water depth. A 7:1 scope in 10 feet of water means 70 feet of rode. Greater scope means a more horizontal pull on the anchor, which increases holding power.

faq.question: What scope ratio should I use?
faq.answer: 5:1 minimum for calm conditions and lunch stops. 7:1 for standard overnight anchoring. 10:1 for storms or high winds. All-chain rode can reduce scope to 5:1 in moderate conditions.

faq.question: Chain vs rope for anchor rode?
faq.answer: Chain is heavier, creating a catenary curve that absorbs shock and keeps pull horizontal. Rope is lighter and easier to handle. Most cruisers use a combination: chain on the bottom (1 boat length minimum) with rope above.

faq.question: Is the Anchor Rode Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard boating and marine formulas trusted by boaters and sailors. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Anchor Rode Calculator?
sections.body: The Anchor Rode Calculator is a free online tool designed for boaters and sailors who need quick, accurate calculations in the boating and marine space. By entering your water depth, bow height above water, scope ratio, you get instant results including total rode needed, recommended chain, swing radius. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total rode needed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In boating and marine, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by boaters and sailors worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to navigate safely with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your water depth and need to find the right total rode needed. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

tips: Double-check your water depth before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
